messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the access-list copy command do?
The access-list copy command copiesan IPv4, IPv6, or MACACLtoanewdestinationACLoroverwritesanexistingACL. Syntax: access-list | {ip|IPv6|mac} | <ACL-NAME> | copy <DESTINATION-ACL>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the access-list copy command?
The syntax of the access-list copy command is: access-list | {ip|IPv6|mac} | <ACL-NAME> | copy <DESTINATION-ACL>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the access-list log-timer command do?
The access-list log-timer command setsthelogtimerintervalforallACEsthathavethelogparameterconfigured. Syntax: access-list | log-timer | {default|<INTERVAL>}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the access-list log-timer command?
The syntax of the access-list log-timer command is: access-list | log-timer | {default|<INTERVAL>}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the access-list mac command do?
The access-list mac command is documented in this guide. Syntax: access-list mac <ACL-NAME>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the access-list mac command?
The syntax of the access-list mac command is: access-list mac <ACL-NAME>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the access-list resequence command do?
The access-list resequence command resequences the ACEsequencenumbersinanACL. Syntax: access-list {ip|IPv6|mac} <ACL-NAME> resequence <STARTING-SEQUENCE-NUMBER> <INCREMENT>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the access-list resequence command?
The syntax of the access-list resequence command is: access-list {ip|IPv6|mac} <ACL-NAME> resequence <STARTING-SEQUENCE-NUMBER> <INCREMENT>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the access-list reset command do?

The access-list reset command changestheuser-specifiedACLconfigurationtomatchtheactiveACLconfiguration. Usethiscommand whenadiscrepancyexistsbetweenwhattheuserconfiguredandwhatisactiveandacceptedbythesystem. Syntax: access-list {all|ip <ACL-NAME>|IPv6 <ACL-NAME>|mac <ACL-NAME>} reset.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the access-list reset command?
The syntax of the access-list reset command is: access-list {all|ip <ACL-NAME>|IPv6 <ACL-NAME>|mac <ACL-NAME>} reset.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the apply access-list control-plane command do?

The apply access-list control-plane command applies an ACL to the specified VRF. The no form of this command removes application of the ACL from the specified VRF. Syntax: apply access-list {ip|IPv6} <ACL-NAME> control-plane vrf <VRF-NAME>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the apply access-list control-plane command?
The syntax of the apply access-list control-plane command is: apply access-list {ip|IPv6} <ACL-NAME> control-plane vrf <VRF-NAME>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the apply access-list (to interface or LAG) command do?

The apply access-list (to interface or LAG) command applies an ACL to the interface (Individual front plane port) or Link Aggregation Group (LAG) identified by the current interface or LAG context. The no form of this command removes application of the ACL from the current interface or LAG identified by the current interface or LAG context. Syntax: apply access-list {ip | IPv6 | mac} <ACL-NAME> {in | out}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the apply access-list (to interface or LAG) command?
The syntax of the apply access-list (to interface or LAG) command is: apply access-list {ip | IPv6 | mac} <ACL-NAME> {in | out}.

For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the class copy command do?

The class copy command copiesaclasstoanewdestinationclassoroverwritesanexistingclass. Copyingaclasscopiesallentriesas well. Syntax: class {ip|IPv6|mac} | | <CLASS-NAME> | | copy | <DESTINATION-CLASS>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the class copy command?
The syntax of the class copy command is: class {ip|IPv6|mac} | | <CLASS-NAME> | | copy | <DESTINATION-CLASS>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the class mac command do?

The class mac command createsormodifiesa MAC trafficclasstomatchspecifiedpackets. Classiscomposedofoneormoreclass entriesorderedandprioritizedbysequencenumbers. Withthiscommand, eachclasscanclassifytraffic basedon MAC headerinformation. The no form of the command can be used to delete either a MAC traffic class (use no with the class command) or an individual MAC traffic class entry (use no with the sequence number). Syntax: class mac <CLASS-NAME>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the class mac command?
The syntax of the class mac command is: class mac <CLASS-NAME>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the class resequence command do?
The class resequence command resequencenumberinginan IPv4, IPv6, or MAC class. Syntax: class {ip|IPv6|mac} <CLASS-NAME> resequence <STARTING-SEQUENCE-NUMBER> <INCREMENT>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the class resequence command?
The syntax of the class resequence command is: class {ip|IPv6|mac} <CLASS-NAME> resequence <STARTING-SEQUENCE-NUMBER> <INCREMENT>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the class reset command?
The syntax of the class reset command is: class { all | ip <CLASS-NAME> | IPv6 <CLASS-NAME>|mac<CLASS-NAME>} reset.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the policy command do?

The policy command Creates or modifies classifier policy and policy entries. A policy is made up of one or more policy entries ordered and prioritized by sequence numbers. Each entry has an IPv4/IPv6/MAC class and zero or more policy actions associated with it. A policy must be applied using the apply command. The no form of the command can be used to delete either a policy (use no with the policy command) or an individual policy entry (use no with the sequence number). Syntax: policy <POLICY-NAME>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the policy command?
The syntax of the policy command is: policy <POLICY-NAME>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the policy copy command do?
The policy copy command copiesapolicytoanewdestinationpolicyoroverwritesanexistingpolicy. Copyingapolicycopiesallits entriesaswell. Syntax: policy <POLICY-NAME> | copy | <DESTINATION-POLICY>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the policy copy command?
The syntax of the policy copy command is: policy <POLICY-NAME> | copy | <DESTINATION-POLICY>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the policy resequence command do?
The policy resequence command resequences numbering in a policy. Syntax: policy <POLICY-NAME> resequence <STARTING-SEQ-NUM> <INCREMENT>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the policy resequence command?
The syntax of the policy resequence command is: policy <POLICY-NAME> resequence <STARTING-SEQ-NUM> <INCREMENT>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the policy reset command do?

The policy reset command changes the user-specified policy configuration to match the active policy configuration. Use this command when a discrepancy exists between what the user configured and what is active and accepted by the system. Syntax: policy <POLICY-NAME> reset.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the policy reset command?
The syntax of the policy reset command is: policy <POLICY-NAME> reset.

For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the apply copp-policy command do?

The apply copp-policy command applies a CoPP policy to the switch, replacing the policy that is in effect. There may be a brief interruption in traffic flow to the management processor while the switch implements the change. Enter the no apply copp-policy <NAME> command with the name of a CoPP policy to unapply a CoPP policy and apply the default CoPP policy. This will only take effect if the specified policy is actively applied. Since there must always be a CoPP policy applied, this command effectively attempts to replace the applied CoPP policy with the default CoPP policy. The default CoPP policy cannot be unapplied using this command. Syntax: apply copp-policy { <NAME> | default }.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the apply copp-policy command?
The syntax of the apply copp-policy command is: apply copp-policy { <NAME> | default }.

For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the guide say about About AOS-CX?

About AOS-CX: AOS-CX is a new, modern, fully programmable operating system built using a database-centric design that ensures higher availability and dynamic software process changes for reduced downtime. In addition to robust hardware reliability, the AOS-CX operating system includes additional software elements not available with traditional systems, including:
- Automated visibility to help IT organizations scale: The Aruba Network Analytics Engine allows IT to monitor and troubleshoot network, system, application, and security-related issues easily through simple scripts. This engine comes with a built-in time series database that enables customers and developers to create software modules that allow historical troubleshooting, as well as analysis of historical trends to predict and avoid future problems due to scale, security, and performance bottlenecks.

The ntp authentication-key command defines an authentication key that is used to secure the exchange with an NTP time server. This command provides protection against accidentally synchronizing to a time source that is not trusted. The no form of this command removes the authentication key. Syntax: ntp authentication-key <KEY-ID> {md5 | sha1}.

For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the ntp server command do?

The ntp server command defines an NTP server to use for time synchronization, or updates the settings of an existing server with new values. Up to eight servers can be defined. The no form of this command removes a configured NTP server. The default NTP version is 4; it is backwards compatible with version 3. Syntax: ntp server <IP-ADDR> [key <KEY-NUM>] [minpoll <MIN-NUM>] [maxpoll <MAX-NUM>][burst | iburst].

For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the show ntp associations command do?

The show ntp associations command shows the status of the connection to each NTP server. The following information is displayed for each server:
- Tally code: The first character is the Tally code: - (blank): No state information available (e.g. non-responding server) o x: Out of tolerance (discarded by intersection algorithm) o.: Discarded by table overflow (not used) o -: Out of tolerance (discarded by the cluster algorithm) o +: Good and a preferred remote peer or server (included by the combine algorithm) o #: Good remote peer or server, but not utilized (ready as a backup source) o *: Remote peer or server presently used as a primary reference o o: PPS peer (when the prefer peer is valid) - ID: Server number. Syntax: show ntp associations [vsx-peer].

For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the show ntp statistics command do?

The show ntp statistics command shows global NTP statistics. The following information is displayed:
- Rx-pkts: Total NTP packets received.
- Current Version Rx-pkts: Number of NTP packets that match the current NTP version.
- Old Version Rx-pkts: Number of NTP packets that match the previous NTP version.
- Error pkts: Packets dropped due to all other error reasons.
- Auth-failed pkts: Packets dropped due to authentication failure.
- Declined pkts: Packets denied access for any reason.
- Restricted pkts: Packets dropped due to NTP access control.
- Rate-limited pkts: Number of packets discarded due to rate limitation.
- KOD pkts: Number of Kiss of Death packets sent. Syntax: show ntp statistics [vsx-peer].

If the specified VLAN exists, this command changes to the config-vlan-id context for the VLAN. If a range of VLANs is specified, the context does not change. The no form of this command removes a VLAN. VLAN 1 is the default VLAN and cannot be deleted.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the no vlan access [<VLAN-ID>] command do?

The no vlan access [<VLAN-ID>] command creates an access interface and assigns an VLAN ID to it. Only one VLAN ID can be assigned to each access interface. VLANs can only be assigned to a non-routed (layer 2) interface or LAG interface. By default, all interfaces are routed (layer 3) when created. Use the no routing command to disable routing on an interface and change the interface to a layer 2 interface. The no form of this command removes an access VLAN from the interface in the current context and sets it to the default VLAN ID of 1.

Up to six hosts can be defined. If no VRF is defined, the default VRF is used. The no form of this command removes a static IP address associated with a hostname. Syntax: ip dns host <HOST-NAME> <IP-ADDR> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ].

For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the clear lldp statistics command do?

The clear lldp statistics command clears all LLDP neighbor statistics. Enables LLDP support globally on all active interfaces. By default, LLDP is enabled. The no form of this command disables LLDP support globally on all active interfaces. It does not remove any LLDP configuration settings. Sets the 802.3 TLVs to be advertised. By default, advertisement of both POE and MAC/PHY TLVs is enabled. Not supported on the OOBM interface. The no form of this command disables advertisement of 802.3 TLVs. Sets the holdtime that is used to calculate the LLDP Time-to-Live value. Time-to-Live defines the length of time that neighbors consider LLDP information sent by this agent as valid. When Time-to-Live expires, the information is deleted by the neighbor. Time-to-live is calculated by multiplying holdtime by the value of lldp timer. Syntax: clear lldp statistics.

For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is High Availability?

High Availability: Key goals of HA include:
- Achieve five-nines (99.999%) availability of switching traffic through minimization of unplanned network outages.
- Fault tolerant: No single active component failure will cause an outage.
- Live replacement of hardware with minimal or no disruption.
Terminology:
- MM: Abbreviation for management module - MM to MM link: Refers to the 10GbE-KR Ethernet link between two MMs - OVSDB: Abbreviation for Open vSwitch Database - Active MM: Management module that has control of the chassis - Standby MM: Backup management module for the active management module - JSON-RPC: Remote procedure call protocol encoded in JSON
Key parts of the HA feature include: Network redundancy: Protocols and redundant network paths provide redundancy in the network, enabling traffic to continue flowing if a network link or network switch fails. Hardware redundancy: Redundant hardware components (power supplies, fabric cards, management modules) allow continued switching traffic or system management in the event of a hardware failure or hardware maintenance.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is Management Module Failover?

Management Module Failover: There are two types of Management Module (MM) failover:
- Controlled failover: The user triggers this type of failover by rebooting the Active MM or running the redundancy switchover command.
- Uncontrolled failover: This type of failover is triggered by unexpected events like a crash on the Active MM or hot removal of the Active MM. In a dual MM chassis, the Standby MM detects failover events in one of the following ways:
- A mailbox interrupt is received from the Active MM to indicate takeover. This interrupt can come for controlled or uncontrolled failover (except for a hot removal).
- Active MM hot removal detection.
- Heartbeat loss detected on the Standby MM for more than 10 seconds. If the Active MM is not responding and is still not detected by the first two methods, it will be caught by this method.
Failover requirements:
- The Standby MM must be present to trigger a failover. An Unassigned MM will never trigger a failover.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the redundancy switchover command do?

The redundancy switchover command causes the switch to immediately switch over to the Standby Management Module. This command must be executed from the Active Management Module and will fail if the Standby Management Module is in a failed state or not present. Syntax: redundancy switchover.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the redundancy switchover command?
The syntax of the redundancy switchover command is: redundancy switchover.

section: Configuring BFD for an IPv4 Static Route

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, how do you configure BFD for an IPv4 Static Route?
To configure BFD for an IPv4 Static Route, follow the documented procedure: 1. Enable BFD support with the command bfd 2. Enable BFD on an IPv4 static route with the command ip route bfd.

task: Configuring BFD for an IPv4 Static Route

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the bfd command do?

The bfd command enables BFD support on the switch. BFD is disabled by default. The no form of this command disables BFD and removes all related configuration settings. To disable BFD, but retain configuration settings, use the command bfd disable. Syntax: bfd.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the bfd command?
The syntax of the bfd command is: bfd.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the bfd <IPv4-ADDR> command do?

The bfd <IPv4-ADDR> command enables BFD under VRRP for the specified IP address. BFD is asynchronous and echo mode is supported. The no form of this command disables BFD under VRRP for the specified IP address. Syntax: bfd <IPv4-ADDR>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the bfd <IPv4-ADDR> command?
The syntax of the bfd <IPv4-ADDR> command is: bfd <IPv4-ADDR>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the bfd all-interfaces command do?

The bfd all-interfaces command enables BFD on all OSPFv2 or OSPFv3 interfaces. The no form of this command disables BFD on all OSPFv2/OSPFv3 or PIM IPv4/IPv6 interfaces, excluding those on which BFD was enabled at the interface level with the commands ip ospf bfd and IPv6 ospfv3 bfd. Syntax: bfd all-interfaces.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the bfd all-interfaces command?
The syntax of the bfd all-interfaces command is: bfd all-interfaces.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the bfd detect-multiplier command do?

The bfd detect-multiplier command sets BFD detection multiplier on an interface. The no form of this command sets the BFD detection multiplier to the default value of 5. Syntax: bfd detect-multiplier | <MULTIPLIER>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the bfd detect-multiplier command?
The syntax of the bfd detect-multiplier command is: bfd detect-multiplier | <MULTIPLIER>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the bfd disable command do?
The bfd disable command disables BFD on the switch, but retains all configuration settings. Syntax: bfd disable.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the bfd disable command?
The syntax of the bfd disable command is: bfd disable.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the bfd enable command do?
The bfd enable command enables or disables BFD for HSC feature. Syntax: bfd enable.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the bfd enable command?
The syntax of the bfd enable command is: bfd enable.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the bfd echo disable command do?

The bfd echo disable command disables support for BFD echo packets. Echo packet support is enabled by default. The no form of this command enables support for BFD echo packets. Toggling this feature on 8325 or 8360 switches may cause route flapping. Syntax: bfd echo disable.

For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the bfd min-receive-interval command do?

The bfd min-receive-interval command sets the minimum time interval between received BFD control packets on an interface. The no form of this command sets the minimum interval between received BFD control packets to the default value of 3000 milliseconds. Syntax: bfd min-receive-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the bfd min-receive-interval command?
The syntax of the bfd min-receive-interval command is: bfd min-receive-interval <INTERVAL>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the bfd min-transmit-interval command do?

The bfd min-transmit-interval command sets the minimum time interval between transmitted BFD control packets on an interface. The no form of this command sets the minimum interval between transmitted BFD control packets to the default value of 3000 ms. Syntax: bfd min-transmit-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the bfd min-transmit-interval command?
The syntax of the bfd min-transmit-interval command is: bfd min-transmit-interval <INTERVAL>

The clear bfd statistics command clears statistics for all BFD sessions or for a specific BFD session. Syntax: clear bfd | statistics | [session | <ID>].

The ip route bfd command enables or disables BFD on the specified static route. To disable BFD, issue the command without the bfd option. Syntax: ip route <DEST-IPv4-ADDR>/<NETMASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR> | <INTERFACE>] [bfd].

The no form of this command sets the current interface to the global settings defined with the command bfd all-interfaces. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 bfd.

The syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 bfd command is: IPv6 ospfv3 bfd.

For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the clear erps ring <ringid> instance <id> command do?

The clear erps ring <ringid> instance <id> command removes the protection switching and triggers reversion both in revertive and non-revertive operation. This command will not change the configured revertive operation mode. Syntax: clear erps ring <ringid> instance <id>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the clear erps ring <ringid> instance <id> command?
The syntax of the clear erps ring <ringid> instance <id> command is: clear erps ring <ringid> instance <id>

The clear erps statistics command this command clears the ERPS statistics for a ring or a ring instance. Syntax: clear erps statistics [ring <id>] [instance <id>].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the clear erps statistics command?
The syntax of the clear erps statistics command is: clear erps statistics [ring <id>] [instance <id>]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the erps ring command do?
The erps ring command this command creates an ERPS ring with a given ID. The no form of this command removes all the configurations of the ring, including instances. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring command?
The syntax of the erps ring command is: erps ring <ringid>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the erps ring <ringid> <port0 port1> interface command do?

The erps ring <ringid> <port0 port1> interface command this command configures the ERPS ring member port. An L2 interface in the switch is associated to one of the two member ports of an ERPS ring. In case of an interconnection node, only port0 is applicable for the sub-ring. The no form of this command removes the association of the ring port to the L2 interface on the switch. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> <port0 port1> interface command?

For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> guard-interval command?

For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the erps ring <ringid> hold-off-interval command do?

The erps ring <ringid> hold-off-interval command specifies hold-off interval in units of 100 ms. If specified, a defect is not reported immediately. Instead, the hold-off timer is started. On expiration of the timer, if the defect still exists, it is reported to protection switching. The default value for hold-off timer is 0. The no form of this command removes the configured value of the hold-off interval and sets it to the default value of 0. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> hold-off-interval command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> hold-off-interval command is: erps ring <ringid>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the erps ring <ringid> instance command do?

The erps ring <ringid> instance command on a common ERPS network, a physical ring can be configured with a single ERPS ring, and only one blocked port can be specified in the ring. When the ERPS ring is in normal state, the blocked port prohibits all service packets from passing through. As a result, all service data is transmitted through one path over the ERPS ring, and the other link on the blocked port becomes idle, leading to ineffective use of bandwidth. To improve link use efficiency, logical rings can be configured in the same physical ring in the ERPS multi- instance. A port may have different roles in different ERPS rings and different ERPS rings use different control VLANs. An ERPS ring must be configured with an ERP instance, and each ERP instance specifies a range of VLANs. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance command is: erps ring <ringid>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> control-vlan command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> control-vlan command is: erps | ring <ringid>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> description command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> description command is: erps | ring <ringid>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> enable command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> enable command is: erps | ring <ringid>

The erps ring <ringid> instance <id> protected-vlans command thiscommandSpecifies thesetof VLANsthatareprotectedbythisringinstance. The no formofthiscommandremovesasetof VLANsthatareprotectedbythisringinstance. Syntax: erps | ring <ringid>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> protected-vlans command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> protected-vlans command is: erps | ring <ringid>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> revertive command do?

The erps ring <ringid> instance <id> revertive command configures the default revertive mode of operation for an ERPS ring. In revertive operation, after the conditions causing protection switching are cleared, traffic channels are restored to the recovered link blocking the RPL. This configuration is meaningful only on the RPL node. The no form of this command configures non-revertive mode of operation for an ERPS ring. In non-revertive operation, the traffic channels continue to use the RPL, if it has not failed, after conditions causing protection switching are cleared. This configuration is meaningful only on the RPL node. Syntax: erps ring <ringid> instance <id> revertive.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> revertive command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> revertive command is: erps ring <ringid> instance <id> revertive.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> role command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> role command is: erps ring | <ringid>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> rpl command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> rpl command is: erps ring | <ringid>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> meg-level command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> meg-level command is: erps ring | <ringid>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> parent-ring command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> parent-ring command is: erps ring | <ringid>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> sub-ring command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> sub-ring command is: erps ring | <ringid>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the erps ring <ringid> tcn-propogation command do?

The erps ring <ringid> tcn-propogation command this command is to configure a sub-ring interconnection node to pass a topology change notification to the ring instance for the parent ring whenever the topology of the sub-ring changes. The parent ring instance performs a Forwarding Database (FDB) flush and sends a protocol message to ensure that other nodes on the parent ring also perform an FDB flush. The no form of this command disables topology change notifications. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> tcn-propogation command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> tcn-propogation command is: erps ring <ringid>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> transmission-interval command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> transmission-interval command is: erps ring | <ringid>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the erps ring <ringid> wtr-interval command do?

The erps ring <ringid> wtr-interval command the RPL owner node uses a delay timer before initiating an RPL block in case of both revertive mode of operation or before reverting to idle state after clearing operator commands (FS, MS). The Wait to Restore (WTR) timer can be configured in 1-minute increments up to 12 minutes. The default value is 5 minutes. When recovering from an SF, the delay timer must be long enough to allow the recovering network to become stable. In the default revertive mode of operation, the WTR timer is used to prevent frequent operation of protection switching due to intermittent SF defects. The no form of this command removes the configured value of the wtr-interval and sets it to the default value of 5 minutes. Syntax: erps ring | <ringid>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> wtr-interval command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> wtr-interval command is: erps ring | <ringid>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the show erps statistics command do?

The show erps statistics command this command displays ERPS statistics. The statistics can be displayed for the ring, the instance, or the instance ports. Syntax: show erps statistics [ring <ringid>] [instance <id> [port0|port1]].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the show erps statistics command?
The syntax of the show erps statistics command is: show erps statistics [ring <ringid>] [instance <id> [port0|port1]]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the show erps status command do?

The show erps status command this command displays detailed information about a specific ring or all instances of a ring. The ring instance may be in one of the following states:
- Idle: The ring instance is operational.
- Initializing: The ring instance is not operational.
- Protection: Protection switching has been triggered by a local or remote link failure.
- Pending: Pending clearance of a previous protection switch.
- Down: Ring instance is not active.
- Manual-switch: Manual protection switching triggered by Admin-down.
- Force-switch: Forced protection switching triggered by admin. A ring instance has the following reasons for "down" state:
- Disabled: Ring instance is administratively disabled. Syntax: show erps status [ring <ringid>] [instance <id>].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the show erps status command?
The syntax of the show erps status command is: show erps status [ring <ringid>] [instance <id>]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the show erps summary command do?
The show erps summary command thiscommanddisplaysasummaryof the ERPSconfigurationandstatefortheERPSringinstances. Syntax: show erps summary.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the show erps summary command?
The syntax of the show erps summary command is: show erps summary.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the diag-dump irdp basic command do?
The diag-dump irdp basic command displaysdiagnosticinformationforIRDP. Syntax: diag-dump irdp | basic.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the diag-dump irdp basic command?
The syntax of the diag-dump irdp basic command is: diag-dump irdp | basic.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the ip irdp command do?

The ip irdp command enables IRDP on an interface and specifies the packet type that is used to send advertisements. By default, the packet type is set to multicast. IRDP is only supported on layer 3 interfaces. The no form of this command disables IRDP on an interface. Syntax: ip irdp [broadcast | | multicast].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the ip irdp command?
The syntax of the ip irdp command is: ip irdp [broadcast | | multicast]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the ip irdp holdtime command do?

The ip irdp holdtime command specifies the maximum amount of time the host will consider an advertisement to be valid until a newer advertisement arrives. When a new advertisement arrives, hold time is reset. Hold time must be greater than or equal to the maximum advertisement interval. Therefore, if the hold time for an advertisement expires, the host can reasonably conclude that the router interface that sent the advertisement is no longer available. The default hold time is three times the maximum advertisement interval. Syntax: ip irdp holdtime <TIME>.

For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the guide say about Configuration consistency requirements?

Configuration consistency requirements:
- Configure at least one active mode aggregation in two devices.
- For a successful static aggregation, make sure the ports at both ends of each link are in the same aggregation state.
- For a successful dynamic aggregation, make sure the peer ports of the ports aggregated at one end are also aggregated, and that one of the ends is configured as "active". The two ends can automatically negotiate the aggregation state of each member port. Removing interfaces - Deleting an aggregate interface also deletes its aggregation group and causes all member ports to leave the aggregation group.
- When a member interface is removed from a LAG: - 6100, 6200, 6300, and 6400 switches: The interface goes to its default status of unshut. - 8320, 8325, 8360, or 8400 switches: The interface becomes disabled. Disabling an interface When an interface LAG is disabled with the shutdown command, all its members also become operationally down. Layer 2 aggregation groups All switches support static and dynamic layer 2 aggregation groups.

For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what restrictions or notes are documented for Guidelines and limitations?

For Guidelines and limitations, the guide documents the following restrictions, notes, or considerations:
- For faster convergence of routed traffic over Smartlink ports, ip neighbor-flood must be enabled on respective SVI interfaces.
- Smartlink uses ERPS copp class for flush packets. Limitations:
- VSX, ISL and MCLAGs cannot be included in Smartlink groups.
- Switches with both Smartlink and STP enabled exclude Smartlink ports from STP.
- On switches with both Smartlink and STP enabled, loops involving Smartlink and STP are not detected.
- On switches with both Smartlink and ERPS enabled, loops involving Smartlink and ERPS are not detected.
- ERPS and Smartlink cannot be enabled on the same port.
- Dynamic VLANs (MVRP) and Smartlink cannot be enabled on the same port.
- Loop Protect and Smartlink cannot be enabled on the same port.
- Multicast fast convergence is not supported.
- Uplink Failure Detection (UFD) is not supported.
- MIB and WebUI are not supported..

For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the apply qos command do?

The apply qos command applies a queue profile and schedule profile globally to all Ethernet and LAG interfaces on the switch, or applies a schedule profile to a specific interface. When applied globally, the specified schedule profile is configured only on Ethernet interfaces and LAGs that do not already have their own schedule profile. The same profile can be applied both globally and locally to an interface. This guarantees that an interface always uses the specified profile, even if the global profile is changed. The no form of this command removes the specified schedule profile from an interface and the interface uses the global schedule profile. This is the only way to remove a schedule profile override from the interface. Syntax: apply qos [queue-profile <QUEUE-NAME>] schedule-profile <SCHEDULE-NAME>.

For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the qos dscp command do?

The qos dscp command configures a differentiated services code point (DSCP) remark for an Ethernet or LAG interface. IPv4 and IPv6 packets that ingress on the interface are remarked at egress using the configured DSCP value. The remark only occurs when QoS trust mode on the interface is set to none. If a DSCP remark is configured and then trust mode is subsequently set to cos or dscp, then the DSCP remark is ignored. The following commands will show the remark status as not applied (incompatible QoS global or port trust configuration): n show running-configuration n show interface <INTERFACE-NAME> n show interface <INTERFACE-NAME> qos The no form of this command removes a CoS remark on an interface. Syntax: qos dscp <CODE-POINT>.

For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the qos queue-profile command do?

The qos queue-profile command creates a new QoS queue profile and switches to the config-queue context for the profile. Or, if the specified QoS queue profile exists, this command switches to the config-queue context for the profile. A queue profile maps queues to local-priority values. Each profile has eight queues, numbered 0 to 7. The larger the queue number, the higher its priority during transmission scheduling. A queue profile named factory-default is defined by default and is automatically applied to all interfaces. It cannot be edited or deleted. Use the command show qos queue-profile factory-default to view this profile. Do not use show running-configuration, as it will only display changes from the initial values. The no form of this command removes the specified QoS queue profile. Syntax: qos queue-profile <NAME>.

If the specified threshold profile exists, this command switches to the config-threshold context for the existing profile. The threshold profile determines the action to take when a threshold is exceeded for each queue. A threshold profile is composed of up to 8 queues, numbered from 0 to 7. Each queue defines the action to take when buffer utilization exceeds a specific threshold. Configure queues with the command queue. The no form of this command removes the specified QoS threshold profile. Only profiles that are not currently applied to an interface can be removed. Syntax: qos threshold-profile <NAME>.

- This command sets the trust mode that is globally applied to all interfaces that do not have a trust mode configured.

Defines the threshold value and action for a queue in a threshold-profile. When queue utilization exceeds the threshold value, ECT (ECN-Capable Transport) packets will be CE (Congestion Encountered) marked when transmitted. Syntax: qos trust {none | cos | dscp}.

section: Introduction to the AOS-CX REST API

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the guide say about Introduction to the AOS-CX REST API?

Introduction to the AOS-CX REST API: The Aruba 6100 Switch Series only supports the default VRF and has no management port. Therefore, references in this guide to other VRFs or the management port do no apply to the 6100 Switch Series. Configuration for these switches should be done over an SVI having a physical port with access to the SVI, since the physical ports in the 6100 are not routed. AOS-CX REST API Switches running the AOS-CX software are fully programmable with a REST (REpresentational State Transfer) API, allowing easy integration with other devices both on premises and in the cloud. This programmability- combined with the Aruba Network Analytics Engine-accelerates network administrator understanding of and response to network issues. The AOS-CX REST API is a web service that performs operations on switch resources using HTTPS POST, GET, PUT, and DELETE methods. The AOS-CX REST API enables programmatic access to the AOS-CX configuration and state database at the heart of the switch.

For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what is VSX solution topology?

VSX solution topology:
- Active gateway support: Active gateways can be configured for active-active routing. VRRP can be used, as an alternative, for active-standby routing.
- ISL links assigned to higher bandwidth: An ISL link has a higher bandwidth compared to VSX links. When planning the topology, consider sizing the ISL link according to the traffic volume required for the east-west traffic of a single-homed VSX during a failover scenario.
- Increasing resiliency: When creating a LAG with multiple ports on each chassis-based switch, it is a best practice to create the LAG with members from multiple line cards. This technique increases the points of resiliency.
- Same VLAN configurations: Both VSX switches have the same VLAN configurations. Make sure that no topology loop is formed because an ISL is added as a member to all the VLANs by default. You can make configuration synchronization automatic between the VSX switches by enabling VSX synchronization.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the guide say about VSX solution requirements?

VSX solution requirements:
- All VSX switches in an environment must have identical settings for the following: - The VLAN membership for all VSX trunk ports. - The loop protection configuration on a VLAN that is part of a VSX LAG.
- Available ports: Make sure that the VSX LAG interface on both the VSX primary and secondary switches has a member port configured and enabled. Make sure that you also have a non-VSX port that is available for the ISL.
- Mutually exclusive features: - VSX active-forwarding and VSX active-gateway on the same VLAN interface.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the guide say about VSX synchronization requirements?

VSX synchronization requirements:
- Software image versions must be the same on both switches.
- The output from the show vsx status command must show in-sync for Config Sync Status.
- Primary and secondary roles configured.
- An interswitch link must be configured.
- When enabling VSX synchronization under a physical interface, a VLAN interface, or a VSX LAG, create on the secondary switch the physical interface, VLAN interface, or VSX LAG with the same name and routing setting as on the primary switch. For example, if the primary switch has a physical interface of 1/1/1, you must create another physical interface of 1/1/1 on the secondary switch. Also, if the primary VSX switch has routing enabled, the secondary switch must have routing enabled. Once the name and routing information is the same, VSX synchronization synchronizes the additional configuration information from the primary VSX switch to the secondary VSX switch. Itisrecommendedto:
- Enablekeepaliveforpreventingtrafficlossduring an ISLlinkfailure.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the guide say about EVPN VSX support?

EVPN VSX support: Ethernet VPN (EVPN) is supported with VSX. The two VSX pairs act as independent BGP routing entities to the other VXLAN tunnel endpoints (VTEPs) or spines for control packets. However, in the datapath, both of them act as a single logical VTEP. This is achieved by using different IP addresses for establishing the BGP session and using a common IP as next-hop to represent the VTEP. For more information on EVPN VSX support, see the EVPN VSX support chapter in the VXLAN Guide..
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the config-sync disable command do?

The config-sync disable command pauses VSX synchronization. The no formofthiscommandrestarts VSX synchronization. AOS-CX10.07Virtual Switching Extension(VSX)Guide Configures a physical port or a LAG as an interswitch link port. Only one port or LAG can be configured to act as an ISL. Once a port is configured as an ISL, it becomes a part of all VLANs in a system. The no form of this command clears the configuration of the interswitch link port from a physical port or a LAG. Sets the dead interval for the interswitch link protocol. The dead interval is the amount of time to wait for hellos from a peer before declaring the peer to be dead. The default dead interval time is 20 seconds. The no form of this command resets the interswitch link dead interval to the default of 20 seconds. Syntax: config-sync disable.

For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the vsx-sync vrrp command do?

The vsx-sync vrrp command enables VSX synchronization of all VRRP configurations on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer switch. There is no configuration sync from secondary to primary peer. To synchronize VRRP configurations at the port level context, the same port must be configured on the peer device with IP address. The no form of this command disables syncing VRRP configurations to the secondary peer, but it does not remove the previously synced configurations from the secondary peer. BFD IP is the IP address of VRRP peer device. Hence it cannot be synced. In the owner scenario, in case the priority is synced, both VSX primary and secondary devices will have 255 as their priority. Syntax: vsx-sync vrrp.

For 8320 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the vsx update-software boot-bank command do?

The vsx update-software boot-bank command upgrades the VSX pairs using the specified bank on both the devices. This command compares whether the image versions are same in both the primary and secondary switches and reboots them in sequence, the VSX secondary switch followed by VSX primary switch. Before executing this command, download the software image and install in the required boot banks. Syntax: vsx update-software boot-bank {primary | secondary}.
